content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Reworked backend default resolution so the implementer and final-review defaults stay on Codex/Opus while preserving workspace and project `backends.<family>.role_models.*` overrides. The compiled defaults now use family-only selections (`workflow.implementer_backend = codex`, `final_review.backends = [codex, claude]`), and the Codex runtime defaults supply `implementer = gpt-5.4-high` plus `final_reviewer = gpt-5.4-xhigh`, allowing project/workspace role-model overrides to win again without duplicating full backend selections.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the effective-config and backend-policy resolution paths to confirm that explicit model-bearing compiled defaults were bypassing `backends.<family>.role_models` overrides for implementers and final reviewers.
2. [Completed] Patch the compiled defaults so implementer and final-review panels default by backend family while moving the Codex default models into backend runtime role-model defaults, preserving workspace/project override precedence.
3. [Completed] Update regression coverage for compiled defaults and workspace/project role-model overrides, then run the required validation commands.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy` passed.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- Added regression tests proving the compiled default implementer now honors workspace/project `backends.codex.role_models.implementer` overrides and the compiled default final-review panel honors `backends.<family>.role_models.final_reviewer` overrides while keeping the default reviewers at Codex `gpt-5.4-xhigh` and Claude Opus 4.6 when no override is set.
- Updated effective-config assertions now verify the compiled defaults expose family-only selections plus the Codex default role models (`backends.codex.role_models.implementer = gpt-5.4-high`, `backends.codex.role_models.final_reviewer = gpt-5.4-xhigh`).

## Outstanding Risks

- The worktree still contains pre-existing unrelated modifications in `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/journal.ndjson` and `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/run.json`, which I left untouched.
- `cargo clippy` still reports existing warning-level findings outside this change set (for example `src/adapters/fs.rs:1122`); the command succeeded.
